name: United Arab Emirates
summary: Day 62 Wednesday. UAE LEAVES OPEC AND OPEC+ EFFECTIVE MAY 1 — STRUCTURAL REALIGNMENT OF GULF PETROLEUM GEOPOLITICS, NOT A QUOTA DISPUTE. The United Arab Emirates announced Tuesday April 28 that it will leave OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, 2026, ending more than five decades of membership (CONFIRMED via The National UAE state media, Washington Post, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Reuters, Bloomberg, Atlantic Council, CFR, Fortune). UAE Energy Minister explicitly: timing 'is now because the Strait of Hormuz is closed and the impact of its exit on the oil market will be limited.' CRITICAL BESSENT-CONTEXT: UAE Central Bank Governor Khaled Mohamed Balama negotiated dollar-swap-line provisions with Treasury Secretary Bessent DAYS BEFORE the announcement — Bessent publicly defended Gulf swap-line requests before US Senate. Deal structure: UAE gets dollar-backstop + top-tier-partnership signal; US gets a major producer unshackled-from-OPEC + committed-to-expanded-output (5M bpd target by 2027) + firmly-inside-American-financial-and-security-architecture. Operational implications: (a) UAE is the second-largest OPEC producer with crucial spare capacity — Saudi Arabia now does more of heavy lifting on price stability and the market loses key shock absorber, (b) Saudi Arabia views this as direct threat to its Gulf-leadership; potential UAE-Saudi rift visible, MBS public silence is itself data (Riyadh's anger), (c) Atlantic Council: UAE absence may pose existential risk to OPEC sustainability over 12-24mo, (d) FOR INDIA: bullish — UAE actively pursuing bilateral oil supply with India/China/South Korea/Japan; PSU refiners IOC/BPCL/HPCL already partner ADNOC on mega-refining projects; Khaleej Times analyst: 'From India's point of view, this is very good development.' UAE position now consolidated as the operational hawkish-on-blockade + diplomatic-on-parliamentary-channel + strategic-autonomy-on-energy axis. The April 11 ADNOC Al Jaber 'Hormuz not open' framing now sits inside a broader strategic-autonomy posture. The structural counterpoint to Brent $115 spot: UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity is a medium-term BEARISH-for-Brent factor (12-24mo) even as today's tape priced near-term-bearish-for-OPEC-coordination = bullish near-term Brent. UAE node may need promotion from Layer 2 to Layer 1 with these new connections (UAE-OPEC exit + UAE-Bessent swap line + UAE-India bilateral oil + UAE-as-Trump-axis-anchor). Domestic war damage backdrop intact: Borouge suspended since April 5; ADNOC Ruwais 922K bpd refinery shut; EGA aluminum damage; Habshan gas complex fire. IMF MENA 1.1% growth projection operative.

signals.content: Iran struck US Consulate in Dubai, Dubai International Airport, and Fairmont The Palm hotel in initial retaliation wave.
ADNOC Ruwais refinery (UAE's largest, 922K bpd capacity) shut down after Iranian drone strike caused fire. Central hub for downstream operations.
Iran struck Shah gas field (set ablaze) and Fujairah port (key oil hub used as Hormuz bypass). UAE activated Abu Dhabi Crude Oil Pipeline to Fujairah on Arabian Sea.

IRGC threatened US university campuses in Gulf on March 29, specifically naming NYU Abu Dhabi. Deadline: Monday March 30 noon Tehran time. Demanded US condemn bombing of Iranian universities or face retaliation against US educational institutions in Gulf. Warned employees, professors, and students to stay 1km from campuses. Represents new category of targeting — civilian educational infrastructure.

IRGC struck Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) facilities. IRGC claimed both ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA had 'ties to US military and aeronautics firms.' The National (Abu Dhabi) confirmed damage. Industrial targeting expansion beyond military and energy. (Sources: The National, Al Jazeera, Arab News)

Iranian drone attacked Kuwaiti tanker Al-Salmi at Dubai Port overnight (March 31, ~12:10 AM). Fire by 4:30 AM. All 24 crew safe. No leakage. One of the most significant maritime attacks — attacking neutral vessel in UAE territorial waters. Undermines selective Hormuz narrative. Insurance must reprice for entire Gulf. UAE described as 'perhaps the most hawkish' among Gulf allies, pushing for ground invasion (WaPo). UAE's 2PointZero sealed $2.25B US deal — resilience messaging. CONFIRMED.

UAE described as 'perhaps the most hawkish' among Gulf allies, pushing for ground invasion of Iran (WaPo national security desk). This is despite: Al-Salmi VLCC attacked at Dubai port anchorage overnight March 31 (most significant single-vessel attack), ADNOC Ruwais shutdown, EGA facilities struck, Dubai airport and consulate hit earlier in war. UAE suffering the most direct infrastructure damage among Gulf states while simultaneously lobbying for escalation. The paradox: UAE's hawkish stance despite damage suggests MBZ calculates long-term strategic gain (crippling Iran) outweighs short-term economic pain. UAE's 2PointZero sealed $2.25B US deal — resilience/normalization messaging. Remote learning extended to April 17 — society-at-war continues. REPORTED (WaPo). (Sources: WaPo, Bloomberg, The National)

Al-Salmi VLCC (Kuwait-flagged, 2M bbl Saudi/Kuwaiti oil for China) struck at Dubai port anchorage March 31 — most significant single-vessel attack of the war, first in allied waters (not Hormuz). EGA Al Taweelah aluminum damage from March 29 still being assessed. DFM/ADX had unprecedented 2-day wartime closure in early March. UAE described as 'most hawkish' Gulf ally pushing Trump to continue fighting (WaPo). (Sources: Bloomberg, Gulf News, Al Jazeera, WaPo)

CONFIRMED: Borouge petrochemical plant ($60B, world's largest single-site polyolefin plant) SHUT DOWN operations at Ruwais Industrial Complex. UAE air defenses intercepted every incoming Iranian missile at 6:44 AM April 5 — but falling debris from SUCCESSFUL interceptions sparked multiple simultaneous fires. Operations immediately suspended. No injuries. This is the paradox of air defense: successful interception shutting down critical infrastructure through debris fallout. In 48 hours before Ruwais, two other facilities (including Habshan gas processing) had already shut from same mechanism. UAE cumulative interception stats: 498+ BMs, 2,141+ drones, 23+ CMs. April 5 alone: 9 BMs, 1 CM, 50 drones engaged. Khor Fakkan port hit by shrapnel — 4 injured (1 Nepali serious, 3 Pakistanis minor). UKMTO reported 'splashes from unknown projectiles' near container ship at port.

UAE domestic war impact materializing: petrol prices jumped ~31% for April (Super 98 from Dh2.59 to Dh3.39/litre), diesel surged 72% (Dh2.72 to Dh4.69). Government announced inflation allowance grants up to Dh900 for families. First time war's energy price impact translated into consumer-facing inflation in a Gulf producer state. Paradox: UAE is an oil exporter whose domestic fuel prices are rising because of war disruption to refining and distribution. Gargash used 'non-negotiable' language on Hormuz freedom — strongest UAE positioning of the war. IRGC designated 18 Western tech companies as 'legitimate targets' and threatened UAE's $500B Stargate AI project. (Sources: Khaleej Times, CNBC, Al Jazeera)

UAE intercepted 9 BMs, 50 drones, 1 CM on April 6 per The National. Borouge operations remain SUSPENDED from April 5 fires — no public restart announcement. The 'successful defense = economic damage' paradox now replicated across UAE (Borouge), Bahrain (Bapco), Kuwait (multiple), and Saudi Arabia (Eastern Province debris). Per WaPo: Gulf allies privately pushing to KEEP fighting — UAE 'most hawkish.' Paradox: Gulf states suffering but lobbying for more war.

EVENING UPDATE: Iran's IRGC threatened $500B Stargate AI data center (OpenAI/SoftBank/Oracle) — military spokesperson Zolfaghari released satellite imagery: 'nothing stays hidden to our sight.' Tasnim confirmed facility within missile range. IRGC broadened threat to data centers of Amazon, Oracle, Nvidia, Apple, Google, Microsoft, Tesla across Gulf. New escalation category: civilian tech infrastructure. Directly threatens UAE economic diversification. Markets ignored earlier IRGC corporate threats (April 1, S&P +2.91%). (Sources: NewsX, Defence Security Asia, Tom's Hardware, CNBC)

UAE intercepted 17 Iranian ballistic missiles and 35 drones within hours of ceasefire taking effect on April 8. Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas complex caught fire. UAE Mirage 2000-9 jets suspected of striking Iran's Lavan Island oil refinery after ceasefire (per Turkiye Today and OSINT analysts) — Abu Dhabi has NOT confirmed or denied. Dr. Gargash declared UAE has 'triumphed in a war it sought to avoid' — the most assertive Gulf reaction. 375 arrests for 'misleading information' during strikes reveals tight information control. (Source: CNBC, Al Jazeera, Gulf News, The National UAE, WAM, Turkiye Today)

Day 44 / Ceasefire Day 4. ADNOC CEO SULTAN AL JABER PUBLICLY DECLARED HORMUZ 'NOT OPEN' [CONFIRMED — most authoritative industry statement]. In an interview picked up by CNBC, Al Jaber said: 'The Strait of Hormuz is not open. Access is being restricted, conditioned and controlled. That is not freedom of navigation. That is coercion.' This is the CEO of UAE's state energy company directly contradicting both the US official line (WH Press Sec Leavitt called Hormuz re-closure 'false'; Defense Sec Hegseth 'the strait is open') AND the Iranian official line (PressTV/Fars: 'open through our managed protocol'). This is the single most authoritative industry statement of the ceasefire period — Al Jaber ran COP28 and has direct relationships with both Trump administration and Iranian counterparts. White House NEC Director Kevin Hassett admitted to Fox Business that traffic is 'about 10% of the normal pace,' still overstating the operational data (5-7 ships/day vs 120-140 normal = ~4-5%). The ADNOC statement matters because it's the first Gulf state energy major to break the diplomatic silence on the ceasefire's Hormuz failure. Implication for UAE position: Abu Dhabi is signaling it will not accept a ceasefire framework that leaves Hormuz under IRGC selective control — reinforcing WaPo's 'most hawkish Gulf ally' framing. Gargash's earlier 'non-negotiable' Hormuz language now has industry CEO backing. For the UAE→strait-of-hormuz edge: weight should strengthen. (Sources: CNBC, The National, Fox Business, Al Jazeera)

Fateh Oil Field explosion and fire observed approximately 45 nautical miles from Jebel Ali, UAE. Causation unclear — could be related to blockade operations, IRGC sabotage, or unrelated industrial accident. No casualties reported. No party has claimed attribution (REPORTED — single source, The War Zone April 13). Separately, UAE named as one of 5 Arab states from which Iran demands reparations for facilitating US-Israeli strikes (REPORTED — Middle East Eye, The National). Houthis specifically warned UAE about potential Bab el-Mandeb closure if Gulf Arab states join the war (source: JPost).

Day 48. UAE CONFIRMED PARTICIPANT IN APRIL 18 UK-FRANCE MULTINATIONAL HORMUZ ESCORT CONFERENCE [CONFIRMED]. A 40+ country video conference will be hosted from Paris on April 18, with confirmed participants including France, Netherlands, Germany, Italy, Canada, Australia, and the UAE. The US is explicitly excluded. Macron described the mission as 'peaceful, multinational, strictly defensive' aimed at 'restoring freedom of navigation.' This is a strategic shift for UAE: simultaneously pursuing the US military pathway (continuing to lobby Trump as 'most hawkish' Gulf ally per WaPo) AND the multilateral insurance/escort pathway. If the April 18 conference produces an operational naval escort commitment with insurance framework, it could directly address the binding constraint on physical oil markets — VLCC transit insurance currently at $10-14M per voyage. UAE's participation signals it views the multinational escort as commercially necessary regardless of the US blockade outcome. Iran has not publicly responded to the initiative. For the investor, this is the third pathway between the US blockade and Iranian selective control that the market has not priced.

CONFIRMED MAJOR DIPLOMATIC SHIFT: UAE Deputy Prime Minister Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan held phone call April 15 with Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf (head of Iran's Islamabad delegation). FIRST high-level UAE-Iran contact since Iran's April 8 salvo (17 ballistic missiles + 35 drones) on Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas complex prompted UAE to recall its Tehran ambassador and close its embassy. Call explicitly focused on 'de-escalation' (WAM framing) — not return to normal relations but bilateral off-ramp from Iran-Gulf retaliatory cycle. Iran has publicly demanded reparations from UAE (among 5 Gulf states); call suggests both sides parking that demand to stabilize immediate tensions. Gulf-Iran backchannel running parallel to Pakistan-mediated US-Iran track — classic multi-track pattern during final stretch before ceasefire renewal decision. Intelligence reading: UAE moving FIRST because Abu Dhabi's economic model (ADIA $1.5T AUM, Dubai transit hub, aviation/tourism) cannot absorb scenario where ceasefire lapses and Iran re-targets Gulf infrastructure. Saudi Arabia has NOT made similar call publicly; MBS quieter than Abu Dhabi on every de-escalation signal of the past week. Both sides confirm call occurred and focus on de-escalation; framings are consistent, suggesting readout was negotiated. (Sources: WAM, Press TV, WANA, Al-Monitor, Rudaw, Xinhua, Tribune India/ANI, Al Bawaba)

CONFIRMED: IMF cut 2026 MENA growth forecast to 1.1% from 3.9% — largest regional growth downgrade IMF has published in 2026 (2.8 pp cut). Drivers include Gulf infrastructure damage from Iranian retaliatory strikes. For UAE specifically: Habshan gas complex fire, Ruwais refinery shutdown, Borouge suspended, Khor Fakkan container port hit. IMF framing: 'could push global economy toward recession' — most alarming institutional assessment of the crisis, exceeds OECD/World Bank language. ~8M Indian diaspora in Gulf remit ~$40B/yr to India; MENA contraction hits Indian remittance-dependent sectors. (Sources: IMF WEO April 2026, Al Jazeera, CNBC)

SECOND UAE-IRAN PARLIAMENTARY CHANNEL CALL IN 72 HOURS [CONFIRMED via Tasnim]. Iran's Tasnim News Agency April 17 reported UAE Vice President Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan phoned Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf to discuss 'regional developments and ways to reduce tensions.' Second such call since April 15 (first since war began). UAE track now parallels Saudi's: both Gulf states maintaining daily Iran contact while publicly urging de-escalation to Washington. UAE also participated in Macron-Starmer Paris Hormuz summit April 17 — one of only 2 Gulf states attending (with Bahrain); Saudi, Qatar, Oman, Pakistan absent. Summit fact-check: 30-40 nations VIRTUAL (CNN: '40-nation virtual'; ABC: 'about 30 countries'), scope 'post-war only' (Macron: 'when security conditions allow'). Two-track UAE posture confirmed: hawkish on blockade (ADNOC Al Jaber 'Hormuz not open' + 'most hawkish' WaPo framing) + diplomatic on parliamentary channel (Mansour-Ghalibaf x2 in 72 hours). (Sources: Tasnim via Al Jazeera aggregated coverage, CNN April 17 liveblog, ABC News)

ADNOC CHAIRMAN AL JABER PUBLIC HARDENING — 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ NOT OPEN, NEEDS TO BE OPEN UNCONDITIONALLY' [REPORTED single-chain Sunday; pending Monday Al Monitor / The National corroboration]. Sultan Al Jaber, ADNOC chairman and former COP28 president, publicly declared Sunday April 19 that the Strait is 'not open' and must be opened 'unconditionally.' Material hardening from morning brief's 'conspicuously quiet' UAE posture — UAE moves from two-track (hawkish-on-blockade Al Jaber + diplomatic Mansour-Ghalibaf parliamentary channel) to publicly hawkish through ADNOC chairman channel. Timing — Sunday before waiver expiry and before any Islamabad Round 2 — suggests UAE pre-positioning a public tough line rather than playing quiet convenor. The National (Abu Dhabi) carries Al Jaber's framing with editorial support; UAE information space aligned against Iran on chokepoint rights. Note: UAE was previously reported (via Times of Israel UAE sourcing) as internally 'pushing for military op to reopen Hormuz, willing to take part' — Sunday Al Jaber moves part of that posture into public record.

CORRECTION TO EVENING BRIEF: AL JABER HORMUZ DECLARATION IS APRIL 9 ADNOC POSITION, NOT A NEW SUNDAY STATEMENT [CONFIRMED via Gulf Business, The National, Al Arabiya, ADNOC press release]. The evening brief framed Al Jaber's "not open and needs to be open unconditionally" language as "new Sunday declaration" and bumped `uae → strait-of-hormuz` edge 7.0 → 8.0 on "first UAE on-record public hardening." Verification shows the language traces to April 9 ADNOC interviews (Gulf Business, Al Arabiya, CNBC, The National, AGBI, CGTN on that date). The UAE posture going into Monday is the same April 9 position — 10-day continuity, not a fresh step-up. Edge weight stands on consistently hardened position, but the proximate phantom-Sunday reason does not survive verification. Graph history should reflect continuity, not step-up.

UAE LEAVES OPEC AND OPEC+ EFFECTIVE MAY 1 — BIGGEST STRUCTURAL OPEC DEVELOPMENT IN A DECADE; BESSENT DOLLAR-SWAP-LINE NEGOTIATED DAYS BEFORE [CONFIRMED via 7+ wires + UAE state media]. The United Arab Emirates announced Tuesday April 28 that it will leave OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, 2026 — ending more than five decades of membership (CONFIRMED — The National UAE [state media], Washington Post 'UAE to leave OPEC amid Hormuz oil crisis', CNBC 'UAE's shock OPEC exit', Al Jazeera, Reuters, Bloomberg, Atlantic Council Apr 28 dispatch, CFR 'Why the UAE Walked Out on OPEC', Fortune, Khaleej Times, Gulf News). UAE stated rationale: 'more freedom of action to make production decisions without the constraints of OPEC' and pursue 5M bpd capacity by 2027. UAE Energy Minister explicitly: timing 'is now because the Strait of Hormuz is closed and the impact of its exit on the oil market will be limited.' Atlantic Council: 'A long time coming.' CRITICAL BESSENT-CONTEXT: UAE Central Bank Governor Khaled Mohamed Balama negotiated DOLLAR-SWAP-LINE PROVISIONS with Treasury Secretary Bessent DAYS BEFORE this announcement (CONFIRMED — Fortune, CNBC, Al Jazeera). Bessent publicly defended Gulf swap-line requests before US Senate. Fortune: 'OPEC shocker as UAE leaves... days after negotiating swap lines with Bessent's Treasury'. Deal structure: UAE gets dollar-backstop + top-tier-partnership signal; US gets a major producer unshackled-from-OPEC + committed-to-expanded-output + firmly-inside-American-financial-and-security-architecture. STRUCTURAL REALIGNMENT, NOT A QUOTA DISPUTE. Operational implications: (a) UAE is second-largest OPEC producer with crucial spare capacity — Saudi Arabia now does more of heavy lifting on price stability; market loses a key shock absorber, (b) Saudi Arabia will view this as direct threat to leadership of Gulf region — Atlantic Council: Riyadh will 'view this action as a new direct threat'; potential UAE-Saudi rift visible in late-2025 Yemen-related tensions; MBS PUBLIC SILENCE through Tue-Wed is itself data (Riyadh's anger), (c) over time, UAE absence may pose existential risk to OPEC sustainability per Atlantic Council, (d) FOR INDIA: BULLISH — UAE actively pursuing bilateral oil supply with India/China/South Korea/Japan; PSU refiners IOC/BPCL/HPCL already partner ADNOC on mega-refining projects. Khaleej Times analyst: 'From India's point of view, this is very good development.' Saudi Arabia formal silence (no MBS public statement Tue-Wed); Iran also silent (UAE OPEC exit happens AT THE SAME TIME as Iran's Hormuz blockade — Iran cannot publicly object given its own market disruption posture). The UAE's two-track posture from earlier weeks (hawkish-on-blockade ADNOC Al Jaber + diplomatic-Mansour-Ghalibaf parliamentary channel) is now consolidated under broader strategic-autonomy-on-energy posture. Brent close $114.64 (+3.04%) Wed — UAE OPEC exit was net-bullish near-term (loss of OPEC management) and is medium-term-bearish-for-Brent (UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity 12-24mo out per Atlantic Council/Derentz). The split-temporal interpretation explains why today's tape priced near-term-bearish-for-management while equity narrative talked medium-term-bullish-for-supply. (Sources: The National UAE, Washington Post, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Reuters, Bloomberg, Atlantic Council, CFR, Fortune, Khaleej Times, Gulf News, Euronews 'Oil prices rise despite UAE exit from OPEC')
signals.verification: CONFIRMED via UAE state media (The National) + 7+ Western wire sources + Atlantic Council/CFR think-tank confirmation + Fortune Bessent-chain confirmation.

edges.context: Day 50 evening. UAE VP Mansour-Ghalibaf SECOND call in 72 hours (Apr 17 via Tasnim). De-escalation focus. UAE parliamentary-speaker channel active parallel to Pakistan-US-Iran track. Two-track UAE: hawkish on blockade (ADNOC Al Jaber) + diplomatic on parliamentary channel (Mansour x2). | 2026-04-19 eve: ADNOC Al Jaber public hardening cuts against Mansour-Ghalibaf parliamentary track.

ADNOC refinery shutdown (922K bpd) directly reduced supply. UAE pipeline bypass partially offsetting Hormuz closure. Domestic fuel prices surging (+31% petrol, +72% diesel) from war disruption. | 2026-04-29: UAE OPEC exit Tue Apr 28 — UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity is medium-term BEARISH-for-Brent factor (12-24mo) but loss of OPEC shock-absorber is near-term BULLISH (today's tape +3.04% to $114.64).

Abu Dhabi Crude Oil Pipeline to Fujairah bypasses Hormuz. But Fujairah itself was attacked, showing bypass not fully secure. Gargash: Hormuz freedom 'non-negotiable' — strongest UAE positioning of war. | 2026-04-19 eve: Al Jaber Sunday 'not open, needs to be open unconditionally' — first UAE on-record public hardening; pending Monday corroboration.

UAE leaves OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, 2026 (CONFIRMED Apr 28 via UAE state media + 6 Western wires + Atlantic Council). Ends 50+ years of membership. Atlantic Council: existential risk to OPEC sustainability over 12-24mo. UAE Energy Minister explicitly: timing 'is now because the Strait of Hormuz is closed and impact will be limited.' Saudi MBS public silence is itself data. Loss of UAE as second-largest OPEC producer + spare capacity = OPEC shock-absorber capacity diminished; Saudi carries more burden. New edge — first-order structural realignment.

Bessent dollar-swap-line negotiated days before UAE OPEC exit (CONFIRMED Fortune, CNBC, Al Jazeera). Balama-Bessent chain establishes UAE inside American financial architecture: UAE gets dollar-backstop + top-tier-partnership signal; US gets producer-unshackled + expanded-output-committed. New edge — first-order financial-architecture leg of Trump-UAE-axis consolidation.

UAE actively pursuing bilateral oil supply with India/China/South Korea/Japan post-OPEC. PSU refiners IOC/BPCL/HPCL already partner ADNOC on mega-refining projects. Khaleej Times analyst: 'From India's point of view, this is very good development.' Re-rating of India energy security narrative — UAE-India bilateral fills part of the Iranian/Saudi gap. New edge — first-order, materially supply-relevant for India.

trigger_points.condition: UAE enters war as combatant (not just defensive)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would massively escalate regional conflict. UAE has capable military. Would divide OPEC. Oil would spike further.

trigger_points.condition: UAE adds 500K-1M bpd of supply within 6 months of OPEC exit (May 1) confirming Atlantic Council/Derentz medium-term-bearish thesis
trigger_points.mechanism: Per Landon Derentz (Atlantic Council VP for Energy): 'Now that the UAE won't be beholden to OPEC production quotas, nothing inhibits the country from leveraging its spare capacity in pursuit of its own strategic interests.' UAE 5M bpd target by 2027 implies ~500K-1M bpd capacity addition runway. Activation criterion: confirmed UAE production data (OPEC March release would be excluded; UAE's own state-data + Bloomberg ship-tracking) showing sustained 500K+ bpd above April 2026 baseline. Falsifiable: 2026-11-01 23:59 IST. ADDED 2026-04-29 at watching.

trigger_points.condition: Saudi Arabia issues formal public response to UAE OPEC exit within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)
trigger_points.mechanism: MBS / Saudi state has been conspicuously silent Tue-Wed on UAE OPEC exit. Atlantic Council: Riyadh will 'view this action as a new direct threat.' Saudi institutional embarrassment is signaled by Al Arabiya quietness. Activation criterion: confirmed Saudi formal statement (MBS, MoFA, Aramco, OPEC delegation) addressing UAE departure. Falsifiable: 2026-05-12 23:59 IST. ADDED 2026-04-29 at watching.
